title: Soehrensia formosa
taxonomy: Soehrensia formosa is classified within the plant kingdom, specifically within the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae and is a part of the tribe Pachycereeae. Soehrensia formosa is further classified into the genus Soehrensia, which is a monotypic genus. This means that it contains only one species, Soehrensia formosa. The classification of Soehrensia formosa is still being refined and updated in the field of botany.

intro: Soehrensia formosa, also known as "Hairy Soehrensia" or "Gilia," is a flowering plant species belonging to the Polemoniaceae family, commonly found in the Andes mountain range in South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate pain and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Soehrensia formosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Soehrensia formosa is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
